steps.title: Use the repeated output
steps.content: The table has $f(24)=-8$ and $f(30)=-8$. A factored quadratic that gives the same output at $x=24$ and $x=30$ is built from $(x-24)(x-30)$, because that product is $0$ at both inputs. To make the output $-8$ at those inputs, use $$f(x)=(x-24)(x-30)-8.$$

steps.title: Use the equal outputs as a quick check
steps.content: The table also shows $f(24)=-8$ and $f(30)=-8$. In $f(x)=(x-24)(x-30)-8$, plugging in $x=24$ or $x=30$ makes the product 0, so both values are $-8$. Then $f(32)=(8)(2)-8=8$, matching the third row.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A: $f(x)=(x-24)(x-30)+4$ gives $f(24)=4 eq-8$. Eliminated.
  • B: $f(x)=(x-24)(x-30)-8$ gives $f(24)=-8$, $f(30)=-8$, $f(32)=8$. All match.
  • C: $f(x)=(x-8)(x-32)+32$ gives $f(24)=(16)(-8)+32=-96 eq-8$. Eliminated.
  • D: $f(x)=(x-8)(x-32)-32$ gives $f(24)=(16)(-8)-32=-160 eq-8$. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
